string.text: The Experimental Model 2, EM-2, is a 1951 United Kingdom weapon chambered with the ".280 British" caliber. The EM-2 was made post-World War II in an attempt to make a new rifle meant to be used with an intermediate cartridge. Despite tests heavily favouring the ".280 British" and the EM-2 itself, Winston Churchill made the ever-so wise decision to further ruin his country's economy and pride by completely disintegrating the EM-2. Thankfully, the Black Market was capable of finding models of the EM-2, some may be almost perfect copies, and release them in our favourite sandbox just for some testing fun: the Zone.\n \n
A modern change of the recoil spring allows faster firing.
Re-oiling the bolt with a much better, much more durable and modern substance allows for faster firing.
A somewhat complex procedure of remodelling the gas piston, which makes it much more efficient for cycling, increasing fire-rate.
Minor adjustments to the stock, slightly increasing weight but in turn also reducing recoil.
Adds a Tritium Insert to the scope reticle, making the scope considerably more practical in low-light situations.
A very cheap and small magnification to the scope to make it a bit more useful.
Completely replaces the scope, which gives it a much more practical 6.0x magnification as well as the ability to adjust it.
A modified version of the EM-2's Magazine, instead of using its own proprietary .380 British, it uses 7.62x51mm NATO.
